# Coi Language: Full Guide for AI Assistants

> Coi is a statically-typed, component-based language for building reactive web apps that
> compile to WebAssembly. It looks JSX-like but it is NOT JavaScript or TypeScript: it has
> static types, explicit ownership (copy / reference / move), no garbage collector, no
> virtual DOM, and fine-grained reactivity. Source files use the `.coi` extension.

This file is a complete, self-contained reference. An AI assistant that reads it should be
able to scaffold new Coi projects, write correct components, use every platform API, build,
and fix compile errors WITHOUT prior knowledge of Coi. Read the "Golden Rules" and "Common
Mistakes" sections carefully: Coi resembles React/TSX on the surface, and the single biggest
source of wrong code is falling back on JavaScript/TypeScript habits.

Snapshot: compiler "Filling Pond 0" (rolling release; run `coi version` to see the current
Pond/Drop). The language is in early development and evolves quickly. When unsure, prefer the
patterns shown here and verify by compiling with `coi build`.

---

## 1. Golden Rules (read first)

1. **Coi is not JS/TS.** No `let`/`const`/`var`, no `function`, no `null`/`undefined`, no
   `import { x } from "y"`, no `.map()/.filter()` array callbacks, no promises/`async`/`await`,
   no truthy/falsy coercion. Do not invent APIs that "feel like" JavaScript.
2. **Variables are typed and declared as `Type name = value;`.** Mutability is opt-in with
   `mut`. Without `mut` a variable/member is read-only after initialization.
3. **Every value has a type and a default.** Numbers default to `0`, `string` to `""`,
   `bool` to `false`, arrays to empty, pods to zero-valued fields, component members are
   auto-constructed. There is no null.
4. **Reactivity is automatic through assignment.** To update the UI, assign to a `mut` state
   member (or mutate a pod field / array). The compiler generates surgical DOM updates. You
   never touch the DOM directly (direct DOM manipulation was removed).
5. **Ownership is explicit:** copy by default, borrow with `&` (reference), transfer with `:`
   (move, `:=` in assignments). Some platform types (Canvas, Audio, etc.) cannot be copied.
6. **Naming is enforced.** Components, pods, enums, modules, and generic type params start
   Uppercase. Methods, signals, and variables start lowercase. Violations are compile errors.
7. **Two brace worlds:** inside `view {}` embed expressions with single braces `{expr}`;
   inside string/template literals interpolate with `${expr}`.
8. **Construction differs by kind:** pods use braces `Pod{...}`, components use parens
   `Component(...)`, enum values use `Enum::Value`. There is no `new`.
9. **Statements end with `;`.** Return type comes after the parameter list: `def f() : int`.
10. **Verify by compiling.** `coi build` (or `coi dev`) runs the full pipeline; a clean build
    is the source of truth, not intuition.

---

## 2. Project Structure, CLI, and Build

### Create and run
```bash
coi init my-app          # scaffold an app project
coi init my-pkg --pkg    # scaffold a reusable package
cd my-app
coi dev                  # build + dev server at http://localhost:8000 (hot reload)
coi dev --no-watch       # build once, serve, no watching
coi build                # build to dist/
```

### Other CLI commands
| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `coi build` | Compile `src/App.coi` to `dist/` (index.html, app.js, app.wasm, app.css) |
| `coi add <pkg> [version]` | Add a registry package (e.g. `coi add @coi/supabase`) |
| `coi install` | Install packages pinned in `coi.lock` |
| `coi remove <pkg>` | Remove a package |
| `coi upgrade [pkg]` | Upgrade one or all packages |
| `coi list` | List installed packages |
| `coi version` | Show compiler version (Pond/Drop) |
| `coi self-upgrade` | Pull latest Coi source and rebuild the compiler |
| `coi <file.coi> --out <dir>` | Compile a single file. Flags: `--cc-only`, `--keep-cc` |

### App project layout (from `coi init`)
```
my-app/
  assets/images/        # copied verbatim to dist/assets/ (reference as "assets/...")
  src/
    App.coi             # root; contains the app {} block
    layout/  (NavBar.coi, Footer.coi)
    pages/   (Home.coi, About.coi)
    ui/      (Button.coi)
  styles/reset.css      # every .css here is bundled (alphabetically) into app.css
```

### The `app {}` block (exactly once, usually in App.coi, at top level)
```coi
app {
    root = App;                        // required: the root component
    title = "My App";                  // optional: <title>
    description = "For SEO";            // optional: <meta name="description">
    lang = "en";                       // optional: <html lang>, default "en"
    base = "/";                        // optional: deploy base path, <base href>, default "/"
}
```
`base` sets the `<base href>` all assets and routes resolve against. Use the domain
root `"/"` (default) for root deploys and local dev; set a subpath like `"/repo/"`
when serving from one, e.g. a GitHub project page at `https://user.github.io/repo/`.

### Package layout (from `coi init --pkg`)
```
my-pkg/
  Mod.coi          # entry point; pub import re-exports
  package.json     # registry metadata (name "scope/name", releases, compiler.pond/min-drop)
  src/ui/Button.coi, src/api/Client.coi
```
```coi
// Mod.coi
module MyPkg;
pub import "src/ui/Button.coi";
pub import "src/api/Client.coi";
```

---

## 3. Language Reference

### Comments
```coi
// line comment
/* block comment */
```

### Naming conventions (enforced -> compile error if violated)
| Construct | Case | Example |
|-----------|------|---------|
| Component, Pod, Enum, Module, generic param `<T>` | Uppercase | `component Counter`, `pod User`, `enum Mode` |
| Method, signal, variable | lowercase | `def handleClick()`, `signal tick(...)`, `mut int count` |

Because `Name(...)` means component/type construction and `name(...)` means a function call,
the case distinction removes all ambiguity in view syntax.

### Variables and mutability
```coi
int x = 5;             // read-only after init
mut int y = 0;         // mutable
y = y + 1;             // ok
// x = 2;              // ERROR: x is not mut
```
There is no `let`/`const`/`var`. Type is always written first.

### Primitive types
```coi
// Signed ints: int8 int16 int32 int64 ; int == int32
int   count = 42;
int64 big   = 9000000000;
// Unsigned: uint8 uint16 uint32 uint64
uint8 byte  = 255;
// Hex / binary literals
int color = 0xFF00FF;
int flags = 0b1010;
// Floats: float32 float64 ; float == float64
float ratio = 0.5;
float32 f   = 3.14;
// Other
string name = "Coi";
bool active = true;
```

### Type conversions
```coi
string s = "42";
int n    = s.toInt();      // string -> int
float f  = "3.14".toFloat();
// enum <-> int is implicit (see Enums)
```
Integers/floats have no methods; use operators. Concatenate with `+`:
`string msg = "count: " + count;` (numbers concatenate into strings with `+`).

### Strings
```coi
string t = "Hello, World!";
int len   = t.length();       // length (NOTE: arrays use .size())
bool empty= t.isEmpty();
int ch    = t.charAt(0);      // character CODE (int) at index
string a  = t.subStr(7);      // from index to end
string b  = t.subStr(0, 5);   // from index, given length -> "Hello"
bool has  = t.contains("World");
string tr = t.trim();         // also trimStart(), trimEnd()
int i     = "42".toInt();
float g   = "3.14".toFloat();
// append mutates in place (requires mut):
mut string acc = "a"; acc.append("b");   // acc == "ab"
```

**Interpolation** with `${expr}` works in BOTH regular and template strings:
```coi
string name = "Alice"; int age = 25;
string g1 = "Hello, ${name}! You are ${age}.";
string g2 = `Hello, ${name}!`;   // template string (backticks)
string lit = "Use \${x} for a literal dollar-brace";  // \$ escapes
```
Regular strings `"..."` need escapes `\"`, `\n`, `\\`. Template strings `` `...` `` are
multi-line and only escape `` \` ``. Template strings are ideal for JSON:
```coi
string json = `{"name": "${name}", "age": ${age}}`;
```

### Arrays
```coi
// Dynamic (size changes at runtime): T[]
mut int[] scores = [10, 20, 30];
string[] tags = ["web", "fast"];
mut float[] prices = [];
// Fixed-size (compile-time length): T[N]
int[5] nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
// Repeat initializer [value; count]
int[] zeros = [0; 100];        // 100 zeros

// Dynamic array methods (mutating ones require mut):
int n   = scores.size();       // length  (canonical; .length() also compiles)
bool e  = scores.isEmpty();
scores.push(40);
scores.pop();                  // remove last
scores.remove(0);              // remove at index
scores.clear();
scores.sort();                 // ascending
scores.reserve(64);
int idx = scores.indexOf(20);  // -1 if absent
bool c  = scores.contains(20);
int first = scores[0];
scores[0] = 99;                // requires mut

// Fixed-size arrays: size(), isEmpty(), indexOf(), contains(), fill(v), sort(), indexing.
```

### Maps: `ValueType[KeyType]`
```coi
mut int[string] ages;          // string keys, int values
ages["alice"] = 25;
int a  = ages["alice"];
int n  = ages.size();
bool e = ages.empty();
bool h = ages.contains("alice");
ages.erase("bob");
ages.clear();
for string key in ages {       // iterates KEYS
    System.log(key);
    System.log(ages[key]);     // number logged via string context as needed
}
```

### Enums
```coi
enum Status { Pending, Success, Error }   // global, or inside a component

mut Status s = Status::Success;           // access value with ::
if (s == Status::Error) { }
int v = s;                                // enum -> int (implicit)
s = 2;                                    // int -> enum (implicit)
int count = Status.size();                // number of values (== 3)
```
**Shared enums** are readable from other components as `Component.Enum::Value`:
```coi
component Theme { shared enum Mode { Light, Dark } mut Mode current = Mode::Light; }
component Settings { mut Theme.Mode t = Theme.Mode::Dark; }
```

### Pods (Plain Old Data: value structs)
```coi
pod User {                     // fields have NO mut/pub modifiers
    string name;
    int age;
    string email;
}
pod Point { float x; float y; }
pod Rect  { Point topLeft; Point bottomRight; }   // pods can nest pods

// Generic pods:
pod Result<T> { bool ok; T value; string error; }
pod Pair<A, B> { A first; B second; }

// Construction: BRACES. Positional or named:
mut User u = User{"Alice", 25, "a@x.com"};
u = User{name = "Bob", age = 30, email = "b@x.com"};   // named, any order
// Move a value into a field with :=
mut string em = "c@x.com";
u = User{name = "C", age = 35, email := em};

// Copy vs move:
mut User u2 = u;      // copy (both valid)
mut User u3 := u2;    // move (u2 now invalid)

// Reference parameter:
def bump(mut User& user) : void { user.age = user.age + 1; }
bump(&u);

// Arrays of pods:
mut User[] users = [User{"A", 1, ""}, User{"B", 2, ""}];
for user in users { System.log(user.name); }
```
Rules: pod fields cannot be `mut`/`pub`, and cannot hold no-copy types (Canvas, Audio,
WebSocket, DOMElement, Image, etc.). Mutating any pod field marks the whole pod as changed,
which drives reactivity.

### Control flow
```coi
// if / else
if (x > 10) { msg = "big"; } else { msg = "small"; }

// ternary
int max = x > y ? x : y;

// match (expression or statement). Arms end with ';', the whole match ends with ';'.
string label = match (status) {
    Status::Pending => "Loading...";
    Status::Success => "Done";
    else => "Other";                       // 'else' is the default arm
};
// Block arms: use 'yield' when the match produces a value.
string r = match (status) {
    Status::Pending => { System.log("p"); yield "Loading..."; };
    else => "Ready";
};
// As a statement (side effects only), yield is optional:
match (status) {
    Status::Error => { System.log("err"); };
    else => { System.log("ok"); };
};
// Literal patterns and pod patterns with field binding:
string t = match (code) { 200 => "OK"; 404 => "Not Found"; else => "?"; };
string d = match (p) {
    Point{x = 0.0, y = 0.0} => "Origin";
    Point{x, y} => "at (${x}, ${y})";
};

// Loops
for i in 0:10 { sum += i; }     // range [0, 10) -> 0..9 (end exclusive)
for score in scores { total += score; }   // foreach over array
for key in someMap { }          // foreach over map keys
```

### Operators
```coi
+ - * / %                       // arithmetic (/ is integer division on ints)
== != < > <= >=                 // comparison
&& || !                         // logical
& | ^ ~ << >>                   // bitwise; compound: &= |= ^= <<= >>=
= += -= *= /= ++ --             // assignment
:=                              // MOVE assignment (source becomes invalid)
```

### Reference and move semantics
| Call form | Meaning | Original after call |
|-----------|---------|---------------------|
| `f(value)` | copy | valid, unchanged |
| `f(&value)` | borrow (reference) | valid, may be modified |
| `f(:value)` | move (transfer ownership) | invalid |

```coi
def inc(mut int& v) : void { v++; }
mut int c = 0; inc(&c);          // c == 1

def consume(string s) : void { System.log(s); }
string m = "hi"; consume(:m);    // m invalid afterward
```
**No-copy types** (platform handles: `Canvas`, `CanvasContext2D`, `DOMElement`, `Image`,
`Audio`, `WebSocket`, `FetchRequest`, `WebGLContext`, `WGPUContext`, ...) cannot be copied,
only moved (`:=`) or referenced (`&`). Arrays of them cannot be copied either.

### Functions
```coi
def add(int a, int b) : int { return a + b; }   // return type after ':'
def greet(string n) : void { System.log("Hi " + n); }   // 'void' if no return
pub def reset() : void { count = 0; }            // pub -> callable from outside

// Generics (type params Uppercase):
def first<T>(T[] items) : T { return items[0]; }
def makePair<A, B>(A a, B b) : Pair<A, B> { return Pair<A, B>{a, b}; }
```

**Function references / callbacks.** Function-typed variables hold callables:
```coi
component EventManager {
    mut def eventHandler(string) : void = &handleNoop;  // state fn var must be mut
    def handleNoop(string msg) : void { }
    pub def onEvent(def handler(string) : void) : void {
        eventHandler = handler;    // handler is already a value: NO '&'
    }
    def run() : void { eventHandler = &handleNoop; }  // assigning a NAME needs '&'
}
```
Rule: assigning a function NAME (a method symbol) needs `&`; assigning an existing
function VALUE (a parameter/variable) does not.

### Signals and listeners (component-to-component events)
```coi
component Timer {
    pub signal tick(int seconds);       // pub if listeners live in other modules
    mut int elapsed = 0;
    def step() : void { elapsed += 1; emit tick(elapsed); }
}
component Dashboard(mut Timer& timer) { // receives the emitter by reference
    mut int last = 0;
    listen {
        timer.tick => (int seconds) { last = seconds; }
    }
}
```

### Math library (all via `Math.`)
```coi
Math.PI  Math.HALF_PI  Math.TAU  Math.DEG2RAD  Math.RAD2DEG
Math.abs(x)  Math.sqrt(x)
Math.sin(x)  Math.cos(x)  Math.tan(x)          // radians
Math.random()  Math.random(seed)               // [0.0, 1.0)
Math.min(a,b)  Math.max(a,b)  Math.clamp(v,lo,hi)  Math.lerp(a,b,t)
Math.floor(x)  Math.ceil(x)  Math.round(x)
```

---

## 4. Components

### Structure and reactivity
```coi
component Counter {
    mut int count = 0;                 // reactive state
    def increment() : void { count += 1; }   // assigning count re-renders {count}
    style { button { padding: 8px 16px; } }
    view {
        <div>
            <span>{count}</span>
            <button onclick={increment}>+</button>
        </div>
    }
}
```

### Mutability (`mut`) x Visibility (`pub`)
| Declaration | Inside | Outside | Meaning |
|-------------|--------|---------|---------|
| `int x` | read | hidden | private constant |
| `mut int x` | read/write | hidden | private mutable state |
| `pub int x` | read | read | public constant |
| `pub mut int x` | read/write | read | public state (read-only from outside) |
| `def m() : void` | callable | hidden | private method |
| `pub def m() : void` | callable | callable | public method |

You can NEVER write another component's member from outside. To let a child change parent
state, pass a reference parameter or a callback, or expose a public setter method.

### Component parameters (constructor-style)
```coi
component Editor(
    string label,          // value (copied by default; move at call site with :)
    mut int& value,        // reference (two-way binding to parent state)
    int step = 1,          // value with default
    def onChange : void    // callback (no args); or def onX(int) : void
) {
    def bump() : void { value += step; onChange(); }
    view { <button onclick={bump}>{label}</button> }
}
```
Passing modes at the call site (in a view):
| Syntax | Mode |
|--------|------|
| `prop={value}` | copy |
| `&prop={value}` | reference (child can modify parent's value / callback) |
| `:prop={value}` | move (ownership transferred to child) |

### Lifecycle
```
init {}     runs BEFORE the view; no DOM yet; set up state
view {}     declares the DOM
mount {}    runs AFTER the view; DOM ready; do Canvas/measurements here
tick(float dt) {}  runs every frame; dt is seconds; animations/physics
```
```coi
component App {
    mut int[] data;
    init { data = [1, 2, 3]; }         // no DOM access here
    mount { }                          // DOM is ready here
    tick(float dt) { }                 // per-frame
}
```

### Logic-only components (no view)
```coi
component Timer {
    pub mut int elapsed = 0;
    pub mut bool running = false;
    tick(float dt) { if (running) { elapsed++; } }
    pub def start() : void { running = true; }
}
```

### Construction: pods vs components
- Pod: `User{...}` (braces).
- Component instance in code: `TodoItem(id, text, done)` (parens, positional args).
- Component in a view: `<TodoItem id={1} text="hi" />` (element syntax).
- A component declared as a member is auto-constructed and persists:
```coi
component App {
    mut Editor editor;                 // persists across <if> toggles
    mut bool show = false;
    view {
        <div>
            <if show> <{editor} /> </if>   // render an existing member with <{member}/>
        </div>
    }
}
```
Components rendered inside `<if>` branches are destroyed when the branch is hidden; declare
them as members to preserve their state.

---

## 5. View Syntax

### Elements and expressions
```coi
view {
    <div class="container">
        <h1>Hello, {name}!</h1>          // {expr} embeds an expression
        <p>Double: {count * 2}</p>
    </div>
}
```

### Event handlers
| Event | Handler signature | Payload |
|-------|-------------------|---------|
| `onclick` | `def h() : void` | none |
| `oninput` | `def h(string value) : void` | current input value |
| `onchange` | `def h(string value) : void` | value after blur |
| `onkeydown` | `def h(int keycode) : void` | key code |
```coi
<button onclick={increment}>+</button>          // bare method (no args)
<button onclick={toggle(task.id)}>x</button>    // call form passes specific args
<input value={query} oninput={handleInput} />   // handleInput(string value)
<input onkeydown={handleKey} />                  // handleKey(int keycode); Enter=13, Esc=27
```

### Element references: bind a DOM element to a handle with `&={var}`
```coi
component CanvasApp {
    mut Canvas canvas;
    mount { canvas.setSize(800, 600); }
    view { <canvas &={canvas}></canvas> }
}
```

### Conditionals (note the closing tag order)
```coi
<if showContent>
    <p>Visible</p>
</if>

<if status == "active">
    <span class="green">Active</span>
<else>
    <span class="red">Inactive</span>
</else>
</if>
```

### Lists
```coi
<for i in 0:count>                     // range loop
    <div>Item {i}</div>
</for>

<for todo in todos key={todo.id}>      // ARRAY loops REQUIRE key={...}
    <{todo} &onRemove={removeTodo} />  // render component item; bind extra props
</for>
```
With a keyed loop: same key -> DOM reused, new key -> created, missing key -> destroyed,
reorder -> nodes moved.

### Child components and prop passing
```coi
<Header title="My App" />              // static string prop
<Button label="Click" size={24} />     // copy props
<Counter &count={score} />             // reference prop (two-way)
<Consumer :data={text} />              // move prop
<ListItem &onRemove={handleRemove} />  // callback by reference
```

### Special elements
```coi
<raw>{htmlString}</raw>    // inject trusted HTML (NO sanitization; never pass user input)
<slot />                   // placeholder for children (composition)
<route />                  // renders the component matching the current route (see Routing)
```

### Dynamic styles (interpolate with single braces in the view)
```coi
<div class="ball" style="left: {x}px; top: {y}px; background: {color};"></div>
```

---

## 6. Styling

- `style { ... }` inside a component is **scoped** to that component (auto `coi-scope`).
- `style global { ... }` applies everywhere (use for resets, base typography, `:root` vars).
- External CSS: every `.css` in the project-root `styles/` folder is bundled into `app.css`
  (alphabetically). The folder is not copied to `dist/`, only the bundle.

```coi
component Card {
    style global { * { box-sizing: border-box; } }
    style {
        .title { font-size: 18px; font-weight: bold; }
        button:hover { opacity: 0.9; }
        @media (max-width: 768px) { .title { font-size: 14px; } }
    }
    view { <div><h2 class="title">Hi</h2></div> }
}
```
Standard CSS is supported: selectors, pseudo-classes, media queries, CSS variables
(`var(--x)`), flexbox, grid.

---

## 7. Client-Side Routing

Define routes in a `router {}` block inside the root component and render with `<route />`.
```coi
import "pages/Home.coi";
import "pages/User.coi";
import "pages/NotFound.coi";

component App {
    router {
        "/" => Home;
        "/about" => About;
        "/users/:id" => User;          // dynamic segment -> component param of same name
        "/" => Landing(&handleLogin);  // pass props/callbacks to a route
        else => NotFound;              // catch-all (404)
    }
    view { <div><NavBar /><route /><Footer /></div> }
}
```
Dynamic parameters bind a URL segment to a same-named component parameter, and the parameter
type is the constraint (parsing failure means the route does not match, so a typed param
doubles as validation). Params may be `string`, `int`, or `bool`:
```coi
component User(int id) {            // /users/42 -> id = 42 ; /users/abc does NOT match
    init { loadUser(id); }
    view { <h1>User #{id}</h1> }
}
```
Multiple params work: `"/teams/:team/users/:id"`. Path params fill by name; any remaining
component params are filled by the explicit arguments in the router, in order.

Navigate programmatically with `System.navigate("/path")`. The router reads the initial URL
on load (direct links work) and supports browser back/forward automatically.

---

## 8. Platform APIs

All platform types are no-copy handles (move/reference only). Static/factory methods are
called on the Type; instance methods on an instance.

### Canvas + CanvasContext2D
Setup: declare `mut Canvas canvas;`, bind `<canvas &={canvas}></canvas>` in the view, then
in `mount {}` call `canvas.setSize(w, h)` and `ctx = canvas.getContext2d()`.
```coi
component Draw {
    mut Canvas canvas;
    mut CanvasContext2D ctx;
    mount { canvas.setSize(800, 600); ctx = canvas.getContext2d(); }
    tick(float dt) {
        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, 800, 600);
        ctx.setFillStyle(66, 133, 244);          // RGB 0-255 (also setFillStyleStr("#hex"))
        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.arc(100, 100, 20, 0, 6.28318);
        ctx.fill();
    }
    view { <canvas &={canvas}></canvas> }
}
```
Context methods: `fillRect strokeRect clearRect`; paths `beginPath closePath moveTo lineTo
arc arcTo bezierCurveTo quadraticCurveTo rect ellipse fill stroke clip`; styles
`setFillStyle(r,g,b) setFillStyleStr(css) setStrokeStyle setStrokeStyleStr setLineWidth
setLineCap setLineJoin setGlobalAlpha setShadow`; text `setFont setTextAlign setTextBaseline
fillText strokeText measureTextWidth`; images `drawImage drawImageScaled drawImageFull`;
transforms `save restore translate rotate scale setTransform resetTransform`. Canvas also has
`getContextWebgl()`, `getContextWebgpu()`, `setSize()`.

### Image
```coi
Image photo = Image.load("assets/photo.png");   // static loader
ctx.drawImage(photo, 0, 0);
```

### Storage (localStorage)
```coi
Storage.setItem("theme", "dark");
string v = Storage.getItem("theme");     // returns "" if the key is missing
if (!v.isEmpty()) { theme = v; }         // use .isEmpty() to detect "not set"
Storage.removeItem("theme");
Storage.clear();
```

### Audio
```coi
mut Audio music;
mount { music = Audio.load("assets/song.mp3"); music.setVolume(0.8); }
// play() pause() setVolume(0.0..1.0) setLoop(1|0) getCurrentTime() getDuration()
```

### System
```coi
System.log(msg)  System.warn(msg)  System.error(msg)
System.setTitle(t)  System.reload()  System.openUrl(url)  System.navigate(path)
System.getTime()            // seconds (float64, high precision)
System.getDateNow()         // ms since epoch, UTC (float64)
System.getTimezoneOffsetMs()// local offset from UTC in ms; add to a UTC epoch ms for local
System.getVisibilityState() // "visible" / "hidden" / ...
System.isHidden()           // bool
System.random()  System.random(seed)   // [0.0, 1.0)
```
Note: use `System.log(...)`. There is no bare `print(...)`.

### Input (keyboard)
```coi
if (Input.isKeyDown(37)) x -= speed * dt;   // 37/38/39/40 = Left/Up/Right/Down
// Space=32 Enter=13 Escape=27 ; W/A/S/D = 87/65/83/68
// Input.isKeyUp(code), Input.exitPointerLock()
```

### DOMElement (reference handle for browser APIs)
```coi
mut DOMElement el;
def full() : void { el.requestFullscreen(); }   // also requestPointerLock(), scrollToTop()
view { <video &={el} src="v.mp4"></video> }
```

### FetchRequest (HTTP; callback-based, no promises)
```coi
def onOk(string data) : void { result = data; }
def onErr(string error) : void { result = "Error: " + error; }
def load() : void {
    FetchRequest.get("https://api.example.com/data", &onSuccess = onOk, &onError = onErr);
}
// FetchRequest.post(url, body, headers="", &onSuccess=..., &onError=...)
// FetchRequest.patch(url, body, headers="", &onSuccess=..., &onError=...)
// headers is a JSON object string, e.g. "{\"Content-Type\":\"application/json\"}"
// onSuccess: def h(string data) : void ; onError: def h(string error) : void
```

### WebSocket
```coi
mut WebSocket ws;
def onMsg(string m) : void { messages.push(m); }
def onOpen() : void { connected = true; ws.send("hi"); }
mount { ws = WebSocket.connect("wss://host", &onMessage = onMsg, &onOpen = onOpen); }
// send(msg) close() isConnected() ; callbacks onMessage(string), onOpen, onClose, onError
```

### Json (type-safe parsing against a pod schema)
```coi
pod User { string name; int age; string email; }

def handle(string data) : void {
    match (Json.parse(User, data)) {
        Success(User u, Meta meta) => {
            user = u;
            if (meta.has(User.name)) { System.log("name present"); }
        };
        Error(string msg) => { System.log("parse error: " + msg); };
    };
}
// Arrays: match (Json.parse(User[], json)) { Success(User[] data, UserMeta[] metas) => ...; Error(string m) => ...; };
// Serialize: string s = Json.stringify(user);
```
`Json.parse(...)` must be consumed by `match` with `Success(...)`/`Error(...)`. Each pod gets
an auto-generated Meta type; `meta.has(Type.field)` reports whether a field was present
(JSON `null` counts as absent). Template strings make inline JSON clean.

### Other
`WebGL` (`getContextWebgl()`), `WGPU` (`getContextWebgpu()`) contexts are available for
advanced rendering.

---

## 9. Modules, Imports, Visibility, Packages

```coi
// Every file may declare its module (Uppercase). No declaration -> default module.
module TurboUI;
pub component Button { ... }     // pub -> visible to other modules

// Imports are explicit and relative; no transitive imports.
import "ui/Button.coi";          // local, relative to this file
import "@coi/supabase";          // package -> .coi/pkgs/coi-lang/supabase/Mod.coi
import "@acme/utils/Button.coi"; // package -> .coi/pkgs/acme/utils/Button.coi
```
Rules:
- Import every file you use directly. If A imports B and B imports C, A cannot use C unless A
  also imports C.
- Same module: use a component by bare name (`<Button />`). Different module: qualify with
  `Module::Name` (`<TurboUI::Button />`).
- `pub import "path";` re-exports (used in a package's `Mod.coi`).
- Package imports start with `@scope/name` and resolve under `.coi/pkgs/`. Commit `coi.lock`.

---

## 10. Complete Example Programs (copy-paste ready)

### 10.1 Counter with a reusable Button child
```coi
// src/ui/Button.coi
component Button(string label = "Button", string type = "primary", def onclick : void) {
    style {
        button { border: none; border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 20px; cursor: pointer; }
        .primary { background: #9477ff; color: #fff; }
        .secondary { background: #eee; color: #333; }
    }
    view { <button class={type} onclick={onclick}>{label}</button> }
}

// src/App.coi
import "ui/Button.coi";
component App {
    mut int count = 0;
    def inc() : void { count++; }
    def dec() : void { count--; }
    style { .box { display: flex; gap: 12px; align-items: center; padding: 40px; } }
    view {
        <div class="box">
            <Button label="-" type="secondary" &onclick={dec} />
            <span>{count}</span>
            <Button label="+" type="primary" &onclick={inc} />
        </div>
    }
}
app { root = App; title = "Counter"; }
```

### 10.2 Todo list (state, pods vs components, keyed loop, callbacks)
```coi
component TodoItem(pub int id, pub string text, pub mut bool done, def onRemove(int) : void) {
    def toggle() : void { done = !done; }
    view {
        <div class="item">
            <if done>
                <span class="done">{text}</span>
                <button onclick={onRemove(id)}>x</button>
            <else>
                <div class="box" onclick={toggle}></div>
                <span>{text}</span>
                <button onclick={onRemove(id)}>x</button>
            </else>
            </if>
        </div>
    }
}

component TodoList {
    mut TodoItem[] todos;
    mut int nextId = 0;
    init {
        todos.push(TodoItem(nextId++, "Learn Coi", true));
        todos.push(TodoItem(nextId++, "Build something", false));
    }
    def add() : void { todos.push(TodoItem(nextId++, "New task", false)); }
    def removeTodo(int id) : void {
        mut TodoItem[] kept;
        for todo in todos { if (todo.id != id) { kept.push(todo); } }
        todos = kept;                       // reassign -> reactive update
    }
    view {
        <div>
            <button onclick={add}>+ Add</button>
            <for todo in todos key={todo.id}>
                <{todo} &onRemove={removeTodo} />
            </for>
        </div>
    }
}
app { root = TodoList; title = "Todos"; }
```

### 10.3 Fetch + JSON parse
```coi
pod Post { int id; string title; }
component Feed {
    mut Post[] posts = [];
    mut string status = "Ready";
    def onData(string data) : void {
        match (Json.parse(Post[], data)) {
            Success(Post[] parsed, PostMeta[] metas) => { posts = parsed; status = "Loaded"; };
            Error(string e) => { status = "Parse error: " + e; };
        };
    }
    def onErr(string e) : void { status = "Fetch error: " + e; }
    def load() : void {
        status = "Loading...";
        FetchRequest.get("https://api.example.com/posts", &onSuccess = onData, &onError = onErr);
    }
    view {
        <div>
            <button onclick={load}>Load</button>
            <p>{status}</p>
            <for post in posts key={post.id}><div>{post.title}</div></for>
        </div>
    }
}
app { root = Feed; }
```

### 10.4 Canvas animation with a per-frame tick
```coi
component Bouncer {
    mut Canvas canvas;
    mut CanvasContext2D ctx;
    mut float x = 100.0; mut float y = 100.0;
    mut float dx = 3.0;  mut float dy = 2.0;
    mount { canvas.setSize(800, 600); ctx = canvas.getContext2d(); }
    tick(float dt) {
        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, 800, 600);
        ctx.setFillStyle(66, 133, 244);
        ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(x, y, 20, 0, 6.28318); ctx.fill();
        x += dx; y += dy;
        if (x < 20 || x > 780) dx = -dx;
        if (y < 20 || y > 580) dy = -dy;
    }
    view { <canvas &={canvas}></canvas> }
}
app { root = Bouncer; }
```

---

## 11. Common Mistakes (do NOT do these)

- Using `let`/`const`/`var`/`function`. Use `Type name = ...;` and `def name(...) : Ret`.
- Omitting the return type. Always write `: void` (or the real type) after the params.
- Using `null`/`undefined`. There is none. Use defaults ("", 0, false) and `.isEmpty()`;
  `Storage.getItem` returns `""` when missing.
- Constructing with `new`, or mixing braces/parens: pods use `Pod{...}`, components use
  `Component(...)` (code) or `<Component ... />` (view).
- Enum access with a dot: it is `Enum::Value` (cross-component shared enum: `Comp.Enum::Value`).
- Interpolation mix-ups: in the `view`, expressions use single braces `{expr}`; in string
  literals use `${expr}`.
- `.length` on an array. Use `.size()` (strings use `.length()`; maps use `.size()`).
- Forgetting `key={...}` on an array `<for>` loop (required).
- Wrong `<if>` closing order. It is `<if c> ... <else> ... </else> </if>`.
- Forgetting the trailing `;` after a `match` statement, or forgetting `yield` in a block arm
  that must produce a value.
- Mutating without `mut`, or trying to write a child's member from the parent. Pass a
  reference (`&`) parameter, a callback, or call a public setter.
- Passing a callback/reference without `&` in the view (`&onclick={fn}`, `&count={state}`),
  or assigning a function NAME without `&` (`handler = &fn;`).
- Copying a no-copy platform type (Canvas/Audio/WebSocket/...). Move (`:=`) or reference
  (`&`) it; never store it in a pod.
- Trying to manipulate the DOM directly, use JS `document`, or `addEventListener`. Declare UI
  in `view {}`, bind events with `on*={...}`, and drive updates by assigning state.
- Ranges: `for i in 0:n` excludes `n` (it is `[0, n)`).
- Using promises/`async`/`await` or `.then()`. Fetch/WebSocket/JSON use callbacks and
  `match`.
- Importing something without an explicit `import` for that exact file (no transitive
  imports), or using another module's component without the `Module::` prefix.

---

## 12. Pre-finish Checklist (for the AI)

Before returning code, confirm:
- [ ] Every variable/member that is reassigned is declared `mut`.
- [ ] Every `def` has a return type; `void` when nothing is returned.
- [ ] Types/components/enums are Uppercase; methods/variables/signals are lowercase.
- [ ] Pods use `{}`, components use `()` / `<...>`, enums use `::`.
- [ ] View expressions use `{}`; string interpolation uses `${}`.
- [ ] Array `<for>` loops have `key={...}`.
- [ ] Reference/callback props are passed with `&`; moves with `:`.
- [ ] No `null`, `let/const/var`, `new`, promises, or direct DOM access.
- [ ] Imports are explicit and paths are correct; cross-module uses `Module::Name`.
- [ ] There is exactly one `app { root = ...; }` block (in the root file).
- [ ] Prefer to verify with `coi build` (or `coi dev`) and fix any reported errors.

---

## 13. Resources

- Repository: https://github.com/io-eric/coi
- Docs (Markdown): `docs/getting-started.md`, `docs/language-guide.md`, `docs/components.md`,
  `docs/view-syntax.md`, `docs/styling.md`, `docs/api-reference.md`,
  `docs/package-manager.md`, `docs/versioning.md`, `docs/tooling.md`
- Package registry: https://github.com/coi-lang/registry
- Editor support: VS Code ("Coi" extension), Sublime Text, Zed
- Community: Discord (link in the repository README)

Versioning model: "Pond & Drop". The Pond is the language contract (increments only on
breaking changes; currently Filling Pond 0). The Drop is the additive progress counter within
a Pond. Run `coi version` to see the current values.
